title: 11 Herbal Creams For Chills
intro_desc: As people age, their bodies undergo many changes that can make them more prone to feeling cold and getting chills. Older adults often experience a decrease in body heat due to a lower metabolism, reduced muscle mass, and poor circulation. This can be especially true for those with certain medical conditions, such as hypothyroidism, diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. When older adults get chills, it's not just a minor annoyance – it can be a sign of a more serious issue. Chills can disrupt their sleep, making them feel tired and groggy, and can also make everyday activities like bathing, dressing, and eating more challenging. Furthermore, chills can be a symptom of a condition like sepsis or a severe infection, which can be life-threatening if left untreated. As a result, older adults need effective and gentle ways to manage chills and stay comfortable.  Herbal creams have become a popular choice for older adults looking to treat chills. These creams often contain natural ingredients that have been shown to help warm the body and promote relaxation. They can be applied directly to the skin, providing a soothing and comforting sensation that can help to reduce chills and promote a sense of well-being. Many of these creams are also free from harsh chemicals and artificial fragrances, making them a good option for older adults with sensitive skin. By using an herbal cream, older adults can take a proactive approach to managing chills and improving their overall quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains compounds like alkylamides, glycosides, and volatile oils that help to combat chills. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with chills. The glycosides, on the other hand, are known to stimulate the immune system, helping your body to fight off the underlying infection that's causing the chills. The volatile oils in Echinacea purpurea creams have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of the infection. If you're experiencing chills, you can try applying Echinacea purpurea cream directly to the affected area, such as your chest or back. Gently massage the cream into your skin until it's fully absorbed. You can also take a warm bath or shower before applying the cream to help loosen up any congestion in your body. As the cream takes effect, you may start to feel a reduction in your body temperature and a decrease in the severity of your chills. You may also notice an improvement in your overall energy levels and a decrease in your body's response to the infection. As the Echinacea purpurea cream continues to work, you may start to feel a sense of relief from the chills and discomfort. Your body's temperature may return to normal, and you may be able to resume your normal activities without feeling weak or tired. If your symptoms persist or worsen, however, you should seek medical attention to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and ease body aches. These constituents, found in the root of the ginger plant, also have natural warming properties that can help combat chills. When you apply Zingiber officinale creams to your skin, these compounds are absorbed, working to increase blood flow and provide relief from cold sensations. To use the cream, apply a small amount to the affected area, such as your arms or legs, and gently massage it in. As the cream is absorbed, you may start to feel a warming sensation and a decrease in chills. This is due to the increased circulation and reduced inflammation caused by the ginger compounds.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains a compound called eucalyptol, which is a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory. When applied topically, eucalyptol helps to relieve chills by breaking down mucus and reducing congestion in the body. This is because eucalyptol has a cooling effect, which can help to lower body temperature and reduce feelings of shivering and chills. To use Eucalyptus globulus creams for chills, apply a small amount to the affected area, such as your chest or back, and gently massage it in. You can also add a few drops of the cream to a warm bath for a more relaxing and soothing experience. When you apply the cream, you may notice a refreshing and cooling sensation, which can help to ease your chills. As the eucalyptol in the cream starts to work, you can expect to feel a reduction in your body temperature and a decrease in your shivering. You may also notice that your breathing becomes easier and less labored, which can help to relieve congestion and make you feel more comfortable. If you're experiencing chills due to a respiratory infection, such as a cold or flu, using Eucalyptus globulus creams may also help to ease your symptoms and support your recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are the key compounds that help alleviate chills. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with chills. The terpenoids, including bilobalide and ginkgolide, improve blood circulation and reduce the constriction of blood vessels, which can help warm the body and alleviate the chills. If you're experiencing chills, try applying a Ginkgo biloba cream to the affected area. Gently massage the cream into your skin, making sure to cover the entire area. You can also take a warm bath or shower before applying the cream to help relax your muscles and increase blood flow. As you apply the cream, focus on taking deep breaths and try to relax, as the heat from your body can help the cream penetrate deeper into your skin. By taking these steps, you can expect to feel a reduction in the severity of your chills. The Ginkgo biloba cream may start to work within 15-30 minutes, and you may feel a gradual warming sensation in your body. As the cream continues to work, you may also experience a decrease in body temperature and a reduction in the discomfort associated with chills. If your chills are caused by a underlying medical condition, consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains licorice root extract, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. It has compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins that help soothe and calm the body, reducing the symptoms of chills. When you apply Glycyrrhiza glabra cream to the affected area, its active ingredients are absorbed into the skin, providing relief from chills by reducing fever and inflammation. To use the cream effectively, apply a thin layer to the area that feels cold or numb, ideally after a warm bath or shower to open up your pores. This helps the active ingredients penetrate deeper into the skin. You can apply the cream 2-3 times a day, or as needed, for quick relief from chills. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra cream regularly, you can expect to see a decrease in the frequency and severity of chills, as well as a reduction in body temperature. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate other symptoms like headaches and fatigue associated with chills.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which are known to have a sedative effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to reduce the body's stress response and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial in alleviating chills. When applied topically, the cream can help to calm the body and mind, reducing feelings of anxiety and discomfort associated with chills.  To use Valeriana officinalis cream for chills, apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin until it is fully absorbed. You can also add a few drops of the cream to a warm bath for a more relaxing experience.  As the cream takes effect, you may notice a decrease in shivering and a sense of calm wash over you. Your body temperature may also begin to regulate, and you may feel more comfortable and relaxed. However, if your chills are severe or persistent, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains properties that help alleviate chills by acting on the body's thermoregulation system. The main constituents, rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce the severity of chills. These compounds also have a direct effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate body temperature. To use Salvia officinalis creams for chills, apply a thin layer to the skin, usually on the wrists or temples, and gently massage it in. You can also add a few drops of the cream to a warm bath or shower to enhance its effects.  When you apply the cream, you may start to feel a slight warming sensation, which is due to the increased blood flow to the skin. This increased circulation can help the body regulate its temperature and reduce the chill. You may also notice that your muscles feel more relaxed, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to the chills. As you continue to use the cream, you may find that your chills become less frequent and less severe. Your body's thermoregulation system may also become more efficient, allowing you to regulate your body temperature more easily. However, it's essential to note that if you have a fever or persistent chills, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains a mix of compounds that help with chills. The main properties of Achillea millefolium include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, thanks to the presence of sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These constituents help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as fever and sweating. If you're experiencing chills, consider applying Achillea millefolium cream to the affected area. To use the cream effectively, follow these steps: gently clean the area, apply a small amount of cream, and massage it into the skin. Repeat the application as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day.  When used correctly, you can expect a reduction in body temperature and a decrease in the frequency and severity of chills. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe any discomfort or pain associated with the condition. However, it's essential to note that Achillea millefolium creams are not a substitute for medical treatment. If your chills are severe or persistent, consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and care.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation, making it easier to manage chills. The menthol in these creams can also help to cool the skin, providing temporary relief from the cold sensation.  When you apply Lavandula angustifolia creams to your skin, the active ingredients are absorbed quickly. They then start to work on the underlying causes of chills, such as muscle spasms and inflammation. To use these creams effectively, apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it into your skin. You can repeat this process as needed, but be sure to follow the instructions on the packaging and start with a small amount to test your tolerance. As the active ingredients start to work, you may notice a reduction in muscle spasms and a decrease in the feeling of coldness. Your skin may also feel soothed and cooled, providing temporary relief from the discomfort of chills. However, it's essential to note that these creams are not a substitute for medical treatment. If your chills persist or are severe, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a key compound that helps reduce chills by acting as a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent. It also contains antioxidants that fight free radicals and promote blood flow, which can help warm up the body. The menthol in these creams can also help stimulate circulation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and feel warmer. When experiencing chills, apply a thin layer of Rosmarinus officinalis cream to your skin, paying special attention to areas like your chest, back, and arms. Gently massage the cream in until it's fully absorbed, and then cover yourself with a blanket to trap the warmth. As the cream takes effect, you should start feeling warmer and more relaxed within 15-30 minutes. Your chills should subside, and you may even feel a sense of calm and reduced anxiety. However, if your chills persist or are accompanied by other symptoms like fever, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol, a key compound that helps with chills. Thymol has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce the growth of germs that cause infections and calm down the body's response to illness. It also contains other constituents like carvacrol and borneol, which contribute to its antiseptic and analgesic effects.  When you apply Thymus vulgaris creams to your skin, the thymol is absorbed into the bloodstream, helping to fight off infections and reduce fever. To get the most out of it, apply the cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day, and gently massage it in. You can also add a warm compress or a hot water bottle on top of the cream to help it penetrate deeper into the skin.  As the thymol and other constituents work their way into your system, you may start to feel a reduction in chills and fever. Your body may start to feel more relaxed, and you may experience a decrease in swelling and pain. You may also start to notice an improvement in your sleep quality and a boost in your energy levels.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for chills

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chills from forming. Certain herbs like ginger and peppermint contain properties that can help warm the body from the inside. When you feel a chill coming on, try sipping on a hot cup of herbal tea to see if it makes a difference. It might just do the trick.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm chills, consuming them every day might not be the best idea. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can cause stomach issues if had excessively. Others, like chamomile, might make you drowsy if taken in large amounts, so moderation is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with chills, but the time it takes to feel better varies. Some people notice a difference in 30 minutes to an hour after drinking a warm tea. Others might feel better within 2-3 hours. It's different for everyone, and it depends on the tea and the severity of the chills.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chills is most effective when your body's core temperature is dropping. This usually happens during the late afternoon or evening hours. So, if you're feeling chilly, brew a cup of herbal tea around 4 or 5 pm, and let its warmth soothe you before bed.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for chills. Some herbs, like ginseng and feverfew, can affect blood pressure or slow blood clotting. This means they might make medications stronger or increase the risk of side effects. It's best to talk to the person taking the medications about any herbal teas they're consuming.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and feeling chilly, herbal teas might sound like a soothing solution. However, some herbs can be risky, like pennyroyal and sage, which can cause uterus contractions. Others, like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger, are usually considered safe, but still, it's best to limit your intake and check the tea's ingredients.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to ease chills, but it's essential to check the ingredients. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, are generally safe for breastfeeding. However, others like pennyroyal and wormwood can be problematic. If you're concerned, choose teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding.
title_2: Chills Got You Down, Older Adults? Try 11 Herbal Creams
